text:
In a town called Whispering Pines, everyone had extraordinary pets. Harold had a weasel named Weepy who could sing songs about historical battles. Lily had a squirrel named Oakley who knew six kinds of gossip. Every day they'd listen to Oakley's gossip while they were outdoors.

One morning, Lily went outdoors looking for Oakley, who hadn't shown up yet. She found Weepy perched on the porch post. "Oh, Oakley," she started, "$ten billion won't talk today."

"Nope," Weepy chirped, striking a pose.

"Lots of stories," Lily mused.

"You got a secret?" Oakley buzzed.

"I could whisper one," Lily said.

"Weepy is afraid his tiny voice might scare a secret away. What was it?" she inquired.

"I once heard somewhere that..." Weepy paused thoughtfully.

"The first king of Rome couldn't hear himself think." Lily finished for him, laughing.

"Dude has great taste
